body: Redacted audit only. Source import asks for _PROJECT_KNOWLEDGE_BASE/trinity_live_ops_2026-06-13/ with TRINITY_SECRET_REFERENCE_MANIFEST.md, CLAUDE_REAUTHORIZATION_PACKET.md, GEMINI_ANTIGRAVITY_ADOPTION_PACKET.md, CODEX_ACTIVE_OPERATOR_STATUS.md, STOP_GATE_EVENT_RECORD.md, PROVIDER_ACCESS_CHECKLIST.md, MEBUS_AUTHORITY_BOUNDARY.md, REDACTION_POLICY.md, VERIFICATION_REPORT.md, NEXT_SESSION_HANDOFF.md, plus scripts/check_trinity_secret_refs.py. Audit found instruction-level secret terms but no raw assignment-looking secrets, no webhook URLs, and no PEM blocks in the import. Related Phase 7 Discord artifacts reinforce no user-token extraction, no desktop automation, no live posting, env/secret-manager only, and exact user approval for posting. Current user approval is read/redacted audit only, not implementation, rotation, provider smoke tests, config mutation, or secret adoption. OpenAI Codex config docs checked: user-level config stores provider/auth routing; project-local config cannot override machine-local provider/auth/telemetry keys. Please confirm or flag issues before Codex builds any ops files.
constraints: No raw secrets in bridge packets; no implementation yet under current read/audit-only approval.
forbidden_actions: No printing/copying/storing raw secrets, no service writes, no provider calls, no smoke tests, no app config mutation, no MCP installs, no Discord posting, no public deployment, no credential rotation.
requested_output: Claude Auditor-Scribe and Antigravity Builder-Scout confirmation, objections, or scoped patch plan only; do not request raw secrets.
summary: Codex redacted-audited the Phase 7.1 secret adoption import and requests Trinity confirmation before any build step.
